homepage.title: Alison McGovern
homepage.text_excerpt: Alison McGovern 0 Skip to Content Alison McGovern Home About News Views Get in Touch Open Menu Close Menu Alison McGovern Home About News Views Get in Touch Open Menu Close Menu Home About News Views Get in Touch My Priorities More homes. More jobs. More to enjoy. These are my priorities for Birkenhead: making sure people can find a place to live, supporting good local employment, and creating opportunities for our community to come together and make the most of what Birkenhead has to offer. Any Questions? How do I get in touch? The best way to contact me is by emailing alison.mcgovern.mp@parliament.uk, filling out the form on this website, or calling my office on 0151 645 6590. Email is preferred as it helps us respond more efficiently. Due to strict parliamentary protocol, I can only help with enquiries from constituents who live in Birkenhead. If you're unsure whether you live within the constituency, you can check here. What if my query relates to your ministerial role? If you're contacting me about my work as Minister of State for Local Government and Homelessness, you'll need to send your correspondence to psalisonmcgovern@communities.gov.uk . My constituency office is unable to advise on or process requests relating to ministerial responsibilities. What can you help with as my MP? I can help with issues relating to government departments and public services, things like housing, benefits, healthcare, asylum cases, or immigration cases. I can't get involved in private disputes, legal matters, or issues with local councils (though I can sometimes help if you've exhausted the council's complaints process) Can I meet with you in person? I hold weekly surgeries for constituents to discuss issues face-to-face. Due to the number of requests we receive, we prioritise surgery appointments based on the urgency and complexity of each case. If you'd like to request a meeting, please contact my office by emailing alison.mcgovern.mp@parliament.uk . Get in Touch alison.mcgovern.mp@parliament.uk Make Hamilton, 69-71 Argyle St, Birkenhead CH41 6LQ Alison McGovern Location Make Hamilton, 69-71 Argyle St, Birkenhead, CH41 6LQ Contact alison.mcgovern.mp@parliament.uk

pages.title: About — Alison McGovern
pages.text_excerpt: About — Alison McGovern 0 Skip to Content Alison McGovern Home About News Views Get in Touch Open Menu Close Menu Alison McGovern Home About News Views Get in Touch Open Menu Close Menu Home About News Views Get in Touch Meet Alison Alison McGovern is the Labour Member of Parliament for Birkenhead, and currently serves as Minister of State for Local Government and Homelessness. She has represented her hometown in Parliament since 2010, first for Wirral South and then for Birkenhead following boundary changes in 2024. When Labour formed a government in July 2024, she was appointed Minister of State for Employment at the Department for Work and Pensions, before moving to her current role at the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government in September 2025. Under Keir Starmer's leadership, she returned to the frontbench in 2020 during the opposition years, leading on labour market policy after three years on the Treasury Select Committee responsible for scrutiny of the UK's monetary and financial institutions. She previously chaired Progress (now Progressive Britain), the organisation for Labour's progressives, and as a pro-European, took a leading role in cross-party backbench liaison on Brexit. Before joining the House of Commons, she was Deputy Leader of the Labour group in the London Borough of Southwark. She is a regular on broadcast and in print on political and economic topics. Outside of politics, she is a season ticket holder at Liverpool Football Club and a regular at Birkenhead Parkrun. She studied Philosophy at University College London and Economics at Birkbeck College London. Alison McGovern Location Make Hamilton, 69-71 Argyle St, Birkenhead, CH41 6LQ Contact alison.mcgovern.mp@parliament.uk
